title: Strengthening Surveillance through Randomized Market Sampling for Salmonella
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Foodborne     illness     caused     by     Salmonella     remains     a     significant     public     health     challenge     worldwide  ,   leading     to     hospitalizations  ,   productivity     losses  ,   and     even     fatalities  .   Randomized     market   sampling   is     a     cost-effective     and     scientifically     robust     strategy     to     detect     contamination     trends     early   in retail   and     distribution     channels  . By testing   a     representative     subset     of     food     products     across     markets  ,   policymakers     can     identify     hotspots  ,   trace     contamination     sources  ,   and     take     timely     corrective     actions  .   This     approach     supports   data-  driven     risk     management     and     ensures     compliance     with     food     safety     standards  .   Strengthening     market     surveillance     also     builds     consumer     confidence     and     enhances     international     trade     credibility  .   Implementing     a     nationwide     randomized   sampling   policy     can     significantly     reduce     Salmonella     outbreaks  ,   improve     outbreak     response     time  ,   and     promote     safer     food     systems     from     farm     to     table  .
